name: United Arab Emirates

visa_free.nationality: GCC Citizens (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia)
visa_free.notes: Citizens of Gulf Cooperation Council member states may enter using passport or national ID card with no visa required. Stay duration is unrestricted for GCC nationals — they are not subject to visa on arrival stamp but enter visa-free under the GCC free movement framework.

Israeli ordinary (biometric) passport holders are visa-free per UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs visa exemptions list. Mutual visa exemption signed October 2021. Laissez passer and passports with extension stickers are not accepted. Stay up to 30 days.

Visa-free entry by mutual agreement (not visa on arrival stamp); ordinary, special, official, and diplomatic passports all included. Maximum 30 days per visit per UAE Embassy Beijing. The prior record erroneously stated 90 days — corrected to 30 days per official MOFA Beijing source.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not on visa-free or visa-on-arrival list
e_visa.notes: Tourist visa available via ICP Smart Services. Continuous stay not exceeding 90 days; the Authority may extend for an equivalent period provided total stay does not exceed 180 days within a single year. Single-entry monthly (AED 100 issuance fee), multiple-entry monthly (AED 200 issuance fee), plus AED 100 application fee and AED 100 smart service fee. Overstay fine: AED 50/day. Requires valid passport, personal photo, tour program statement, and valid UAE health insurance.

visa_policy.notes: The GDRFA Dubai official page lists nationalities eligible for visa on arrival (stamped at airport, no advance application needed). GCC nationals (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia) enter visa-free using passport or national ID, with no fixed stay limit. Israel ordinary passport holders are visa-free (up to 30 days) per the MOFA official visa exemptions page — confirmed by the UAE–Israel mutual visa exemption signed October 2021; laissez passer and extension-sticker passports are not accepted. China: 30 days visa-free for ordinary, special, official, and diplomatic passports per the mutual agreement confirmed on the UAE Embassy Beijing page; the prior 90-day figure has been corrected. All other nationalities typically apply for e-visa online prior to travel. The GDRFA list may not be exhaustive — additional bilateral agreements may apply.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa — 30 Days Single Entry
visa_types.notes: Applied online via ICP portal or through UAE airlines/hotels. Valid 60 days from issue; 30-day stay from entry. Extendable once for 30 days (fee applies). Some nationalities receive visa on arrival free of charge.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa — 60 Days Single Entry
visa_types.notes: Applied via ICP portal, airlines, or authorized travel agents. Valid 60 days from issue; 60-day maximum stay. Extendable.

visa_types.name: 5-Year Multiple Entry Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Repeated tourism and short visits over 5 years
visa_types.notes: Maximum 90 days per visit. 5-year validity from date of issue. Available to eligible nationalities. Applied via ICP portal.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa / Work Permit
visa_types.notes: Employer-sponsored. Requires job offer from UAE-registered employer. MOHRE work permit + ICP residence visa. Initial 2-year validity, renewable. Fee varies by emirate and employer type.

visa_types.name: Remote Work / Digital Nomad Visa
visa_types.purpose: Working remotely for overseas employer while residing in UAE
visa_types.notes: 1-year renewable visa for employees of foreign companies earning min USD 3,500/month net. Must show employment contract, 3-month bank statements, health insurance. Applied via remotework.ae. Covers freelancers and company employees. Dubai-specific program.

visa_types.name: Golden Visa (Long-term Residence)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residence for investors, entrepreneurs, skilled professionals, students
visa_types.notes: 10-year renewable residence visa. Categories: investors (AED 2M+ real estate or AED 2M+ company capital), entrepreneurs (startup founders with approved project), specialized talents (doctors, scientists, athletes, artists, inventors), outstanding students (GPA 3.8+). No UAE sponsor required. Self-sponsored. Issued by ICP via online portal.

visa_types.name: Retirement Visa — 5 Years
visa_types.notes: For retirees aged 55+. Requirements: monthly income of AED 20,000+, or savings of AED 1M+, or UAE property worth AED 2M+. Renewable 5-year visa. Issued through ICP portal. No sponsor required.

conditional_access.conditions: Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid US permanent resident card (Green Card) with at least 6 months validity from arrival date. Passport must also have at least 6 months validity. Visa on arrival valid for 14 days; extendable once for an additional 14 days. Fee: AED 253. Apply online via smart.gdrfad.gov.ae (processing: 48 hours). Confirm requirements with airline before travel.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid UK residence visa with at least 6 months validity from arrival date. Passport must also have at least 6 months validity. Visa on arrival valid for 14 days; extendable once for an additional 14 days. Fee: AED 253. Confirm requirements with airline before travel.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid EU country residence visa with at least 6 months validity from arrival date. Passport must also have at least 6 months validity. Visa on arrival valid for 14 days; extendable once for an additional 14 days. Fee: AED 253. Confirm requirements with airline before travel.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid US visa (any category) with at least 6 months validity from arrival date. Passport must also have at least 6 months validity. Visa on arrival valid for 14 days; extendable once for an additional 14 days. Fee: AED 100. Confirm requirements with airline before travel.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid residence permit from Australia. Effective 13 February 2025 per official UAE MOFA New Delhi announcement. Visa on arrival at any UAE entry point for tourism or business; stay up to 14 days (extendable once for 14 days). Study and work require pre-arrival visa. Prior record stated 60 days — corrected to 14 days per confirmation that the GDRFA terms apply to all qualifying credential holders.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid residence permit from Canada. Effective 13 February 2025 per official UAE MOFA New Delhi announcement. Visa on arrival at any UAE entry point for tourism or business; stay up to 14 days (extendable once for 14 days). Study and work require pre-arrival visa.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid residence permit from Japan. Effective 13 February 2025 per official UAE MOFA New Delhi announcement. Visa on arrival at any UAE entry point for tourism or business; stay up to 14 days (extendable once for 14 days). Study and work require pre-arrival visa.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid residence permit from New Zealand. Effective 13 February 2025 per official UAE MOFA New Delhi announcement. Visa on arrival at any UAE entry point for tourism or business; stay up to 14 days (extendable once for 14 days). Study and work require pre-arrival visa.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid residence permit from Republic of Korea (South Korea). Effective 13 February 2025 per official UAE MOFA New Delhi announcement. Visa on arrival at any UAE entry point for tourism or business; stay up to 14 days (extendable once for 14 days). Study and work require pre-arrival visa.

Indian ordinary passport holder must hold a valid residence permit from Singapore. Effective 13 February 2025 per official UAE MOFA New Delhi announcement. Visa on arrival at any UAE entry point for tourism or business; stay up to 14 days (extendable once for 14 days). Study and work require pre-arrival visa.

Pakistani holders of diplomatic and official passports are exempt from UAE entry visa requirement per the UAE–Pakistan mutual visa waiver MoU signed June 24, 2025, and activated effective July 25, 2025. Does not apply to ordinary Pakistani passport holders. Confirmed active at all UAE airports. Stay duration not specified in available official announcement.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in UAE. The UAE offers long-term residency (Golden Residency, Green Residency, Blue Residency) but not citizenship through investment. Citizenship is granted only by presidential decree in exceptional cases. No official CBI program was found on icp.gov.ae, gdrfad.gov.ae, or mofaic.gov.ae.

programs.name: Golden Residency (Investors in Public Investments)
programs.notes: 10-year renewable residency. Options: (1) Company investment/shares valued at min AED 2 million; (2) Local bank deposit (Sukuk or investment bonds) of min AED 2 million; (3) Annual tax contribution of min AED 250,000. No sponsor required. Family sponsorship included. Does not lead to citizenship.

programs.name: Golden Residency (Real Estate Investor)
programs.notes: 5-year renewable residency for real estate investment (10-year per GDRFA route for fully-owned properties). Property must be fully owned, minimum value AED 2,000,000. Processing: 2 days. Service fee: AED 2,790 (GDRFA Dubai route).

programs.name: Golden Residency (Entrepreneurs)
programs.notes: 5-year renewable residency for entrepreneurs. Requires valid commercial/industrial license with Articles of Association (capital not less than AED 2 million) OR Federal Tax Authority letter confirming annual tax payments of at least AED 250,000. Project must be innovative/technological. Confirmed current per ICP Golden Residency page (March 2026).

programs.name: Green Residency (Skilled Workers)
programs.notes: 5-year renewable self-sponsored residency. Requires valid UAE employment contract, skill levels 1-3 per MOHRE occupational classification, minimum bachelor's degree, and monthly salary of AED 15,000 or more.

programs.name: Green Residency (Freelancers/Self-Employed)
programs.notes: 5-year renewable self-sponsored residency. Requires freelancing/self-employment permit from MOHRE, minimum bachelor's degree or specialized diploma, and annual freelance income of at least AED 360,000 over past two years.

programs.name: Blue Residency (Environmental Contributors)
programs.notes: 10-year renewable residency for individuals who have made exceptional contributions to environmental protection and sustainability inside or outside UAE. Categories include: environmental influencers, scientists/researchers, environmental investors/entrepreneurs, inventors/innovators, elite specialists, members of international organisations/companies/NGOs, and global award winners. No fixed minimum investment amount confirmed on official u.ae page — prior AED 1 million figure for 'investors' category is unconfirmed on the live official page and has been removed. No sponsor required.

programs.name: Virtual Work Residence (Remote Work Visa)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals employed remotely outside UAE with minimum monthly salary of USD 3,500 or equivalent in foreign currency
programs.notes: Allows remote workers to legally reside in UAE. Requires proof of remote employment outside UAE, salary certificate. Total fees: AED 300. Overstay fine: AED 50/day.

programs.name: Golden Residency for Specialized Talents
programs.eligibility: Exceptional talents in Arts & Culture, Innovation & Invention, Athletics, Digital Technology, or other fields recognized by federal/local government authorities. Digital technology talents require approval from UAE Council for Artificial Intelligence.
programs.notes: 10-year Golden Residency for exceptional talents. Requires government recommendation/approval per category. Total fees: AED 300. Covers arts, innovation, sports, digital technology, and other exceptional fields.

programs.name: AI Specialists Entry Visa
programs.eligibility: Artificial intelligence specialists sponsored by a technology-focused establishment in UAE
programs.notes: New visa category for AI specialists. Single or multiple entry within a specified period. Sponsoring entity must be a technology-focused establishment.

programs.name: Golden Residency for Outstanding Students
programs.eligibility: High school graduates with cumulative GPA of at least 95% (or equivalent); OR university graduates with GPA of at least 3.5 (Classification A) or 3.8 (Classification B) within 2 years of graduation from UAE universities
programs.notes: 5-year residency for high school graduates (95%+), 10-year residency for top UAE university graduates. GPA thresholds corrected per live ICP page (March 2026): Classification A requires GPA 3.5+, Classification B requires GPA 3.8+. Prior record stated 3.8+ for all university graduates — corrected to reflect tiered classification.

agreements: UAE–China mutual visa exemption (ordinary, special, official, diplomatic passports — 30 days)
UAE–Pakistan mutual visa waiver for diplomatic and official passports (effective July 25, 2025)

validation_notes: CORRECTION: GCC nationals moved from visa_on_arrival to visa_free — they do not receive a visa on arrival stamp; they enter via free movement framework using passport or national ID with no fixed stay limit.

CORRECTION: Israel added to visa_policy.visa_free (up to 30 days) sourced from mofa.gov.ae/en/visa-exemptions-for-non-citizen (official MOFA visa exemptions page). Prior record only mentioned Israel in notes without classifying it; now correctly classified as visa_free.

CORRECTION: China max_stay_days corrected from 90 to 30 days, per UAE Embassy Beijing official page (mofa.gov.ae/en/missions/beijing/services/visas). The bilateral agreement covers ordinary, special, official, and diplomatic passports for 30 days — not 90 days.

CORRECTION: India conditional access for Australia/Canada/Japan/New Zealand/South Korea/Singapore residence permits: max_stay_days corrected from 60 to 14 days. The official MOFA New Delhi announcement (mofa.gov.ae) does not state the duration; the GDRFA service page specifies 14 days extendable once. The prior 60-day figure was from a non-official immigration law firm source and has been replaced.

ADDITION: Pakistan diplomatic/official passport visa waiver added to conditional_access (basis=passport_type) per multiple corroborating news sources confirming activation on July 25, 2025. Marked source_official=false as no direct UAE MOFA announcement URL was accessible — sourced from Dawn.com (Pakistani national newspaper) and corroborating Gulf News report.
CORRECTION: Blue Residency min_amount removed (set to null). The prior AED 1 million figure for the environmental investor sub-category was not confirmed on the current official u.ae Blue Visa page.

CORRECTION: Golden Residency for Outstanding Students eligibility updated to reflect tiered GPA classification per live ICP page: Classification A (GPA 3.5+) and Classification B (GPA 3.8+). Prior record stated a single 3.8+ threshold.

CORRECTION: Entrepreneur Golden Residency notes updated — the capital requirement is AED 2 million for commercial license route OR AED 250,000 annual tax (confirmed per ICP search results), not AED 500,000 project value as prior record stated. This was a significant error.

CORRECTION: e-visa notes updated to reflect current ICP page wording: continuous stay up to 90 days (extendable to 180 total in a year). Prior description of '30-day and 60-day options at AED 200 and AED 300' is no longer reflected on the live ICP page.
ADDITION: agreements array updated to include UAE-Israel, UAE-China, and UAE-Pakistan bilateral agreements.
gaps: Pakistan diplomatic/official passport waiver (effective July 25, 2025) confirmed by multiple news sources but no direct UAE MOFA official announcement URL was accessible to verify; recorded with source_official=false.
Stay duration for Pakistan diplomatic/official passport holders not specified in available sources.
Israel stay duration (30 days) inferred from the mutual exemption agreement terms — the MOFA visa exemptions page listing does not specify duration explicitly per the fetched content.
GCC nationals' exact stay duration is not specified on the official page (unlimited/unrestricted implied by national ID entry rules).
Complete list of e-visa eligible nationalities is not publicly enumerated on official pages — only the VOA list and the MOFA exemption list are enumerated; all others are implied e-visa required.
Path to citizenship from residency programs is not officially stated on ICP pages — UAE citizenship is granted only by presidential decree; no official pathway duration published.
UAE does not have a formal CBI program; absence confirmed by absence on official sites but no official statement explicitly denying one was found.

No Schengen visa holder conditional entry rule found on any official UAE source — the GDRFA/ICP pages do not reference Schengen Type C visa as a qualifying credential; only EU residence permit is confirmed.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific conditional entry rule for UAE found on any official UAE government source.
Blue Residency minimum investment amount for environmental investor sub-category: AED 1 million figure from prior record is unconfirmed on current official u.ae page and has been removed.
The GDRFA Dubai list (gdrfad.gov.ae/en/node/2086) may not reflect all bilateral agreements — the MOFA exemptions page lists more countries; a comprehensive merged list was not compiled.
Minimum permit validity requirements for Australia/Canada/Japan/New Zealand/South Korea/Singapore residence permits (Indian passport holders) not stated in the official MOFA announcement.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs interactive travel requirements map at mofa.gov.ae/en/travel-updates, queried per-country using the ?code= parameter. Data reflects normal (ordinary) passport holder category. Israel is listed as visa-free but requires an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA-IL) before travel via israel-entry.piba.gov.il — classified as visa_free per the MOFA page label. Thailand requires a Thailand Digital Arrival Card (TDAC) before arrival but is classified as visa_free per MOFA. Lebanon is technically visa-free but a UAE travel ban to Lebanon is in effect. USA requires a visa (visa required). This list covers countries checked; the MOFA page contains ~195 countries — not all were individually queried. Countries not listed here should be verified directly at mofa.gov.ae/en/travel-updates.
